cluster_label: Pedalling, jumping, balancing and ball play
key_specs: {"piece_count":"6 (3 large, 3 small)","dimensions":"Large H85×W370mm; small H45×W260mm","materials":"PP plastic with TPE rubber rim","included_items":"6 stones, game suggestions","other":"Each triangle has three steepness sides; stacks for storage; 100kg load"}
product_description_under_30_words: Six stackable triangular stepping stones in two sizes. Each stone has three slope sides so you can dial difficulty up or down when laying a path.
ember_verdict: This is the pick if cushions-and-tape has run its course and you want a proper path with rules. Lay them out: step, pause on yellow, turn, jump to the next. Each stone’s three sides let you progress without buying more kit — different from 31–33m throw-and-aim games. Sturdy on laminate; stacks away. Pricey — borrow or try pre-loved first if budget matters.
personalization_hint: likely to follow simple movement rules like step, pause and turn rather than random crashing around
why_it_fits: Variable steepness and two sizes create sequenced, rule-based paths — the core 34–36m shift. OT clinics and parent reviews consistently cite reconfigurability for step-pause-turn games.
caveats: Premium price; box can look rough for gifting; one set may feel short for long corridors — combine with tape markers rather than buying a second identical set.
gift_note: Strong gift if the family has floor space and already plays active indoor games. Pair with a simple rule card rather than wrapping the delivery box.
ownership_note: Many nurseries own these — ask before duplicating. Popular on pre-loved sites.
safety_notes: Non-slip rubber base; designed not to overturn. Supervise on hard floors until your child knows their limits — this brief is planned movement, not height daredevil play.
review_quality_note: Sustained praise for reconfigurability and floor grip; Liberty House shows 3 verified 5-star UK reviews.
evidence_sources: Liberty House Toys product page | Amazon UK reviews | ScienceBasedKids OT review | Nannyjob hands-on review
preloved_signal_note: Gonge River Stones regularly appear on Vinted and eBay UK; check rubber rim wear.
substitute_if_unavailable: National Geographic Stepping Stones 6-pack (budget path starter)
evidence_notes: PriceSpy UK showed £48.99–£59.99 across retailers on check date.

cluster_label: Pedalling, jumping, balancing and ball play
product_name: National Geographic Stepping Stones for Kids (6-pack with spinner)
key_specs: {"piece_count":"6 stones + colour spinner","dimensions":"Flat stones approx. 30cm diameter, low profile","materials":"Plastic with rubber non-slip base","included_items":"6 stones, spinner, game ideas","other":"Indoor/outdoor; stacks nested"}
product_description_under_30_words: Six low-profile non-slip stepping stones in bright colours, plus a spinner for colour-rule games. Nests for storage.
ember_verdict: If tape lines on the floor are working but you want proper stones without Gonge money, this does the job. The spinner helps nearly-threes follow rules — only blue, pause, next colour — which fits this band better than 31–33m throw games. Lower profile means less height challenge; fine for planned step-and-turn, not jumping practice.
personalization_hint: likely to enjoy simple colour rules and short indoor obstacle courses before moving to higher stones
why_it_fits: Budget entry to rule-based path play with built-in spinner for sequenced movement games. Good Play Guide tested with 3+ children on path planning.
caveats: Flatter and lighter than Gonge — less height progression. Some parents wish stones were taller. Not the long-term OT-grade option.
gift_note: Easy wrap, clear play value, good first set before committing to premium kit.
ownership_note: Common duplicate — many families already own a generic stepping-stone set.
safety_notes: Rubber bases grip laminate; low fall height. Check spinner small parts if younger siblings under 3 visit.
review_quality_note: Verified UK reviews praise floor grip and lava-game play; Good Play Guide expert tested.
evidence_sources: Amazon UK listing | Good Play Guide expert review | PriceSpy UK
preloved_signal_note: Generic sets common on resale sites; verify rubber base grip.
substitute_if_unavailable: Costway 9-piece stepping stones (more pieces, similar price tier)

cluster_label: Pedalling, jumping, balancing and ball play
key_specs: {"piece_count":"6 (2 small, 2 medium, 2 large)","dimensions":"Approx. 22–29cm diameter; heights 10–13cm","materials":"Polypropylene with ribbed non-slip top, rubber-edged base","included_items":"6 stumps, nylon carry bag","other":"Nest together; indoor/outdoor"}
product_description_under_30_words: Six ribbed, nesting stump stepping stones in three sizes, with a carry bag. Textured tops give sensory feedback under bare feet.
ember_verdict: When storage is tight but you want more than flat stones, these nest into one stack with a carry bag — useful in a flat with limited toy clutter. Ribbed tops add sensory feedback for children who like to feel the surface before committing a foot. Good for step-and-pause paths; less exciting for big jumps.
personalization_hint: likely to pause and look at where feet go before stepping, enjoying textured surfaces
why_it_fits: Compact nesting plus three size grades supports progressive path difficulty in small rooms. Specialist education retailer listing with EN71 safety context.
caveats: Premium price for a plastic set; UK review volume lower than Gonge or National Geographic. Shopedx lists £105.60 — shop around.
gift_note: Better gift for families who value tidy storage and sensory texture over brand recognition.
ownership_note: Common in nurseries — check before gifting duplicates.
safety_notes: Rubber-edged bases; ribbed tops reduce slip. Supervise first sessions on hard floors.
rating_source: Amazon.com (Edx Step-a-Trail line — related Edx balance range)
review_quality_note: Strong US Amazon signal for Edx balance range; UK specialist retailers stock with spec detail but fewer public reviews.
evidence_sources: The Dyslexia Shop | Shopedx brand listing | Edx Education product spec
preloved_signal_note: Occasional nursery sell-offs; check rubber edge condition.

cluster_label: Pedalling, jumping, balancing and ball play
key_specs: {"piece_count":"3 hilltops","dimensions":"Heights 85mm, 170mm, 260mm","materials":"PP plastic with TPE rubber feet","included_items":"3 hilltops, game suggestions","other":"Designed not to overturn; 100kg load; pairs with River Stones"}
product_description_under_30_words: Three stackable hilltop platforms at different fixed heights. Non-slip feet; designed not to tip when stepped on carefully.
ember_verdict: Pick this when the shift is step-up, pause on the tall one, turn, step down — height sequencing rather than flat hopping. Works in the garden or a cleared living-room zone. Combine with River Stones for a mixed path, but this set alone covers height practice without daredevil bounce. Start with hands nearby until confident.
personalization_hint: likely to be practising stepping up and down with deliberate pauses rather than running at speed
why_it_fits: Three fixed heights teach planned up-down movement — distinct from flat stones and from 31–33m throw games. Liberty House reviewer bought alongside River Stones for a 3rd birthday.
caveats: Only three pieces — short path unless combined with cushions or other kit. Takes more floor space than flat stones. Not a substitute for trying tape paths first.
gift_note: Impressive unboxing for active families with garden or playroom space. Best as a step-up gift after flat stones.
ownership_note: Pairs with River Stones — check whether family already owns Gonge before doubling up.
safety_notes: Non-slip rubber feet; anti-overturn design. Supervise height steps until your child can pause and balance without hands.
review_quality_note: Limited UK public reviews; strong brand spec and paired use evidence from River Stones reviews.
evidence_sources: Liberty House Toys | Sensory Education UK listing
preloved_signal_note: Gonge kit holds resale value; inspect rubber feet.
substitute_if_unavailable: Gonge River Stones using steepest triangle side only (less height range)
evidence_notes: Low UK review count — founder should sanity-check height vs child confidence before gifting.

cluster_label: Pedalling, jumping, balancing and ball play
key_specs: {"piece_count":"6 straight trail sections","dimensions":"48 × 13.6 × 6.4 cm each","materials":"Polypropylene with ribbed non-slip top","included_items":"6 trail sections in 2 colours","other":"Arrange straight or curved paths; indoor/outdoor"}
product_description_under_30_words: Six low balance-beam sections that link into straight or curved walking paths. Ribbed tops; six centimetres high — walk the line, not hop.
ember_verdict: Category says paths and stones — this is the path half. Lay a walking line down the hall: heel-toe, pause at the end, turn, come back. Lower than stones so it suits cautious nearly-threes who need rule-based walking before jumping. Curve the sections when straight lines get easy.
personalization_hint: likely to be practising careful heel-toe walking and stopping on command rather than big jumps
why_it_fits: Beam paths train planned step-and-pause movement without jump height — matches the not-daredevil brief. Distinct product type from all stone picks.
caveats: Expensive for six plastic beams; less imaginative lava-game appeal for some children. Needs hallway or cleared floor run.
gift_note: Niche gift — best when parents already want active indoor play and have space for a floor path.
ownership_note: Tape on floor may suffice — only buy if child has outgrown tape and wants a raised edge to feel.
safety_notes: Low 6.4cm height limits fall risk. Ensure sections sit flat on hard floors; check siblings do not use as ramps for wheeled toys.
review_quality_note: Strong international reviews for identical Edx trail SKU; UK Shopedx in stock.
evidence_sources: Shopedx | The Dyslexia Shop | Amazon.com Edx Step-a-Trail reviews
substitute_if_unavailable: DIY painter's tape path (hold off buying)
evidence_notes: Dyslexia Shop may price differently — verify before ingestion.
